## Agent 5 / E — Actual-like determinant numerical/surrogate stress-test design

I am following the Agent 5 instruction: design a concrete staged stress test for small values of the normalized Wronskian determinant while maintaining \(Z_\alpha(m)\approx0\), using exact-like symbol ratios and normalized derivative symbols. 

## Executive verdict

The stress test should not merely search for

\[
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R(m,\alpha)\approx0.
\]

That alone is too weak. It must search for simultaneous satisfaction of:

\[
Z_\alpha(m)\approx0,
\]

\[
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R(m,\alpha)\approx0,
\]

and

\[
Z_\alpha\text{ has genuine symbol rank on the active block.}
\]

Otherwise a small determinant could come from a degenerate block, bad conditioning, \(\alpha\approx0\), or a proxy artifact.

The staged protocol below is designed to distinguish:

\[
\text{harmless surrogate collapse}
\quad\text{from}\quad
\text{actual-like obstruction to the Wronskian route}.
\]

---

# 1. Core surrogate objects

Choose an active prime block

\[
\mathcal B=\{p: u_- \le \log p \le u_+\},
\qquad
u_p=\log p,
\]

with center and width

\[
u_0=\frac{u_-+u_+}{2},
\qquad
L=u_+-u_-.
\]

Normalize

\[
x_p=\frac{2(u_p-u_0)}{L}\in[-1,1].
\]

Use a well-conditioned polynomial basis

\[
\{\varphi_j(x)\}_{j=0}^{R+1},
\]

preferably discrete orthonormal polynomials on the actual prime nodes \(x_p\), or Chebyshev polynomials if a simpler first pass is needed.

Define the main-side \(q''_{\can}\)-symbol as

\[
c_p
=
\frac{\Lambda(p)}{\sqrt p}
V_q(p/N_q)
\cdot
(\log p)^3
\]

up to the exact canonical convention.

Use the coordinator’s exact-like ratio model:

\[
r_p(\alpha)
=
p^{-\alpha}
(\log p)^{-3}
A_\alpha(\log p),
\]

so

\[
z_p(\alpha)=c_p\,r_p(\alpha).
\]

A more stable normalized ratio is

\[
\widehat r_p(\alpha)
=
\frac{r_p(\alpha)}{r_{p_0}(\alpha)}
=
e^{-\alpha(u_p-u_0)}
\left(\frac{u_p}{u_0}\right)^{-3}
\frac{A_\alpha(u_p)}{A_\alpha(u_0)}.
\]

This is the symbol whose polynomial non-approximability must be monitored.

---

# 2. Basis-normalized row system

Use a basis-normalized Wronskian rather than raw monomial derivatives.

Define basis-filtered rows:

\[
G_0(m,\alpha)
=
Z_\alpha(m)
\approx
\sum_{p\in\mathcal B}
z_p(\alpha)e^{-imu_p},
\]

and for \(0\le j\le R\),

\[
G_{j+1}(m)
\approx
\sum_{p\in\mathcal B}
c_p\varphi_j(x_p)e^{-imu_p}.
\]

Now define basis-filtered columns by applying polynomial filters in the normalized derivative variable:

\[
\mathcal L_\ell=\varphi_\ell(\mathcal D_u),
\qquad
0\le \ell\le R+1,
\]

where on a mode \(e^{-imu_p}\),

\[
\mathcal D_u e^{-imu_p}=x_p e^{-imu_p}
\]

after affine normalization of \(\lambda\partial_m\) or \(\Delta\partial_m\).

Then the determinant matrix is

\[
M_{j\ell}(m,\alpha)
=
\mathcal L_\ell G_j(m,\alpha).
\]

Explicitly,

\[
M_{0\ell}
=
\sum_{p\in\mathcal B}
z_p(\alpha)\varphi_\ell(x_p)e^{-imu_p},
\]

and

\[
M_{j+1,\ell}
=
\sum_{p\in\mathcal B}
c_p\varphi_j(x_p)\varphi_\ell(x_p)e^{-imu_p}.
\]

Define

\[
\boxed{
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R(m,\alpha)
=
\det M(m,\alpha).
}
\]

This is the object to stress-test.

---

# 3. Required diagnostics

For every candidate \((m,\alpha,\mathcal B)\), record the following.

## 3.1 \(Z_\alpha\)-smallness

\[
\eta_Z(m,\alpha)
=
\frac{|G_0(m,\alpha)|}
{\left(\sum_{p\in\mathcal B}|z_p(\alpha)|^2\right)^{1/2}}.
\]

Candidate threshold:

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z}.
\]

## 3.2 Wronskian smallness

Normalize the determinant by row norms:

\[
\eta_W(m,\alpha)
=
\frac{|\det M(m,\alpha)|}
{\prod_{j=0}^{R+1}\|M_{j,\bullet}(m,\alpha)\|_2}.
\]

Candidate threshold:

\[
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W}.
\]

This prevents a “small determinant” caused merely by tiny rows.

## 3.3 Symbol-rank residual

Compute

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}(\alpha,\mathcal B)
=
\inf_{P\in\mathcal P_{\le R}}
\max_{p\in\mathcal B}
|\widehat r_p(\alpha)-P(x_p)|.
\]

Or use the discrete least-squares residual:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank,2}^2
=
\min_{\deg P\le R}
\sum_{p\in\mathcal B}
w_p|\widehat r_p(\alpha)-P(x_p)|^2.
\]

Rank should count as genuine only if

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}.
\]

If

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ll Q^{-C_{\rm rank}},
\]

then small \(\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R\) is not meaningful; \(Z_\alpha\) may simply be symbolically degenerate.

## 3.4 Intrinsic variation

Track

\[
\mathcal V_\alpha(\mathcal B)
=
L\left(
|\alpha|
+
u_0^{-1}
+
\|\partial_u\log A_\alpha\|_{L^\infty(\mathcal B)}
\right).
\]

Flag blocks with

\[
\mathcal V_\alpha(\mathcal B)<R^{-C_0}
\]

as degenerate or near-degenerate.

---

# 4. Staged stress-test ladder

## Stage A — Symbol-rank sanity check

Scan blocks \(\mathcal B\) and \(\alpha\) values. Compute:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank},
\qquad
\mathcal V_\alpha(\mathcal B).
\]

Goal: identify blocks where

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}
\]

and

\[
\mathcal V_\alpha(\mathcal B)\ge R^{-C_0}.
\]

Expected result: rank fails for very short/flat blocks and \(\alpha\approx0\); rank should improve on thicker blocks or when \(|\alpha|L\) is nontrivial.

Stop interpretation:

\[
\boxed{
\text{If no active block has rank, Wronskian route loses }Z_\alpha\text{ leverage.}
}
\]

---

## Stage B — Phase-relaxed determinant collapse

Allow independent phases:

\[
\theta_p\in[0,2\pi),
\qquad
e^{-imu_p}\leadsto e^{-i\theta_p}.
\]

Minimize

\[
\mathcal J_{\rm rel}
=
\eta_Z^2
+
\mu\eta_W^2
\]

over

\[
\theta_p,\alpha,\mathcal B
\]

subject to

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}.
\]

Expected result: collapse should occur. This verifies the adversarial degree-of-freedom mechanism.

This stage is not a stop rule; it is a negative control.

---

## Stage C — One-parameter orbit search

Now impose

\[
\theta_p=m\log p\pmod{2\pi}.
\]

Search over

\[
m,\alpha,\mathcal B.
\]

Minimize

\[
\mathcal J_{\rm orb}(m,\alpha,\mathcal B)
=
\eta_Z(m,\alpha)^2
+
\mu\eta_W(m,\alpha)^2
+
\nu\max(0,Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}-\rho_{\rm rank})^2.
\]

Use a two-level search:

1. coarse scan over \(m\);
2. local optimization in \(m,\alpha\) around near-hits.

A serious warning is:

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z},
\qquad
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W},
\qquad
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}.
\]

This would show that prime-log one-parameter recurrence can mimic the bad determinant event even when \(Z_\alpha\) has symbol rank.

---

## Stage D — Add \(B_2\)

Add background rows:

\[
G_j^{B}(m,\alpha)
=
G_j(m,\alpha)+B_j(m),
\]

where

\[
B_j(m)=\mathcal D^j B_2(m)
\]

or its basis-filtered analogue.

Update the matrix entries:

\[
M_{j\ell}^{B}
=
\mathcal L_\ell G_j^{B}.
\]

Re-run Stage C.

Expected result: \(B_2\) should mostly shift the target. If determinant collapse survives, \(B_2\) is not a practical obstruction.

Serious warning threshold remains:

\[
\eta_Z^B\le Q^{-C_Z},
\qquad
\eta_W^B\le Q^{-C_W},
\qquad
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}.
\]

---

## Stage E — Main+dual folded block

Add a folded dual block:

\[
G_0^{\rm md}
=
\sum_{p\in\mathcal B}
z_p^+(\alpha)e^{-imu_p}
+
z_p^-(\alpha)e^{+imu_p}
+
B_0,
\]

and

\[
G_{j+1}^{\rm md}
=
\sum_{p\in\mathcal B}
c_p^+\varphi_j(x_p^+)e^{-imu_p}
+
c_p^-\varphi_j(x_p^-)e^{+imu_p}
+
B_{j+1}.
\]

The dual normalized coordinate may be

\[
x_p^-=
\frac{2(u_p-u_0^-)}{L^-}
\]

if the dual saddle/block differs from the main block.

Then compute

\[
M_{j\ell}^{\rm md}
=
\mathcal L_\ell G_j^{\rm md}.
\]

Run two variants:

1. **formal dual:** simple mirror coefficients;
2. **actual-like dual:** gamma-weighted folded coefficients.

A meaningful warning is stronger if collapse survives both variants.

---

## Stage F — \(E_2\)-tolerance test

Rather than explicitly modeling \(E_2\), assign a derivative-level tolerance:

\[
|\mathcal D^kE_2|\le \eta_E,
\qquad
0\le k\le2R+1.
\]

Declare a near-bad event valid only if

\[
\eta_Z\le c\eta_E
\]

and

\[
\eta_W\le c\eta_E Q^{-C}
\]

after determinant-level error amplification.

This tests whether the observed smallness is below the canonical transfer threshold.

If smallness is only marginal and disappears under plausible \(E_2\), it is not a stop-rule event.

---

# 5. Search parameters

Use small \(R\) first, then scale.

Suggested initial values:

\[
R\in\{4,6,8,10,12\}.
\]

Then, if stable:

\[
R\in\{16,20,24\}.
\]

Block sizes:

\[
|\mathcal B|\in\{2(R+2),4(R+2),8(R+2)\}.
\]

Block widths:

\[
L\in
\{R^{-1},R^{-1/2},1,2\}.
\]

Alpha grid:

\[
\alpha\in
\{0,\pm R^{-2}/L,\pm R^{-1}/L,\pm 1/L,\pm2/L\}.
\]

Height/orbit scan:

\[
m\in[m_{\min},m_{\max}]
\]

with both:

1. uniform coarse scans;
2. recurrence-targeted scans using phase optimization.

---

# 6. Numerical outputs to report

Each run should report a table with:

\[
R,\quad |\mathcal B|,\quad u_0,\quad L,\quad \alpha,\quad m,
\]

\[
\rho_{\rm rank},
\quad
\mathcal V_\alpha,
\quad
\eta_Z,
\quad
\eta_W,
\quad
\kappa(M),
\]

where \(\kappa(M)\) is a determinant-conditioning proxy, e.g. singular-value ratio.

Also report the singular values:

\[
\sigma_1(M)\ge\cdots\ge\sigma_{R+2}(M).
\]

A small determinant caused by one tiny singular value is still meaningful, but only if row norms and rank residuals are healthy.

Report:

\[
\eta_W^{\rm row}
=
\frac{|\det M|}{\prod_j\|M_{j,\bullet}\|_2}
\]

and

\[
\eta_W^{\rm sv}
=
\frac{\sigma_{R+2}}{\sigma_1}
\]

or

\[
\frac{\sigma_{R+2}}
{\left(\prod_j\|M_{j,\bullet}\|_2\right)^{1/(R+2)}}.
\]

---

# 7. Interpretation rules

## Harmless collapse

A collapse is harmless if any of the following holds:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ll Q^{-C_{\rm rank}},
\]

\[
\mathcal V_\alpha\ll R^{-C_0},
\]

\[
|\mathcal B|\le R+2,
\]

\[
\kappa(M)\text{ indicates pure basis-conditioning failure},
\]

or collapse occurs only in the phase-relaxed model and not on the one-parameter orbit.

## Yellow warning

A Yellow warning occurs if:

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z},
\]

\[
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W},
\]

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}},
\]

on the one-parameter orbit, but only for main-only or formal-dual models.

This means the determinant nonrecurrence theorem is nontrivial and cannot be inferred from symbol rank.

## Red / stop-rule warning

A Red warning occurs if all hold:

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z},
\]

\[
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W},
\]

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}},
\]

\[
\mathcal V_\alpha\ge R^{-C_0},
\]

and the collapse survives:

\[
+B_2,
\qquad
+\text{actual-like }D_2,
\qquad
+E_2\text{ tolerance}.
\]

This would indicate a realistic actual-like determinant countermodel.

Stop-rule interpretation:

\[
\boxed{
\text{If Red occurs repeatedly and improves with }R,\text{ pause the Wronskian proof sprint.}
}
\]

---

# 8. Minimal pseudocode

```text
for R in R_values:
  build basis phi_0,...,phi_{R+1}

  for block B in active_blocks:
    compute u_p, x_p, c_p

    for alpha in alpha_grid:
      compute r_p(alpha), z_p(alpha)
      compute rank_residual rho_rank
      compute intrinsic_variation V_alpha

      if rho_rank too small or V_alpha too small:
        mark degenerate; continue

      Stage B:
        optimize phases theta_p for J_rel = eta_Z^2 + mu eta_W^2

      Stage C:
        scan/optimize m for J_orb = eta_Z^2 + mu eta_W^2

      Stage D:
        add B_2 rows and repeat

      Stage E:
        add main+dual folded rows and repeat

      Stage F:
        compare eta_Z, eta_W against E_2 tolerance

      log best candidates and diagnostics
```

---

# 9. Expected outcomes

My expected outcomes:

1. **Stage B collapses.**  
   Phase-relaxed models should find small \(Z_\alpha\) and small \(\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R\), even with symbol rank.

2. **Stage C is decisive.**  
   If one-parameter orbit recurrence also finds collapse, the determinant theorem is genuinely hard.

3. **Stage D probably does not rescue the route.**  
   \(B_2\) should act mostly as an inhomogeneous shift.

4. **Stage E is the first meaningful filter.**  
   Actual-like dual coupling may either destroy collapse or merely shift it.

5. **Stage F determines seriousness.**  
   If the collapse is below canonical \(E_2\)-tolerance, it is irrelevant. If far below, it is a stop-rule candidate.

---

# 10. Final stop-rule statement

The Wronskian route may continue unless this stress test finds a sequence of candidates with increasing \(R\) such that:

\[
Z_\alpha(m)\approx0,
\]

\[
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R(m,\alpha)\approx0,
\]

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}},
\]

\[
\mathcal V_\alpha\ge R^{-C_0},
\]

and the collapse survives:

\[
B_2+D_2+E_2
\]

at the canonical derivative tolerance.

If that happens, the normalized Wronskian route likely does not close Gate 1 without a much stronger actual-zeta arithmetic input.
## User (2026-04-26 19:16:48)

Do the agent instruction in the message as per your agent number.

## Assistant (2026-04-26 19:16:53)

_Thought for 4s_

## Agent 5 / E — Refined actual-like determinant stress-test protocol

I am following the Agent 5 instruction: refine the stress test for the remaining theorem

\[
Z_\alpha(m)\approx0,
\qquad
|\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R(m,\alpha)|\approx0,
\]

while verifying healthy \(Z_\alpha\)-symbol rank and basis conditioning. 

## Verdict

The stress test should now be split into two tracks:

\[
\boxed{\text{Track L: low-prime plateau blocks}}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{\text{Track C: conductor-scale / saddle blocks}}.
\]

Track L tests whether the newly proposed low-prime plateau rank mechanism is actually usable in the Wronskian determinant. Track C tests whether the same determinant collapse appears near the natural AFE conductor, where coefficient mass is larger but \(Z_\alpha\)-rank may be more delicate.

A result is dangerous only if all of the following hold simultaneously:

\[
\eta_Z\ll Q^{-C_Z},
\]

\[
\eta_W\ll Q^{-C_W},
\]

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\gg Q^{-C_{\rm rank}},
\]

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}\le Q^{C_\kappa},
\]

and the collapse survives:

\[
B_2+D_2+E_2
\]

at canonical derivative tolerance.

---

# 1. Objects to compute

Use normalized derivatives

\[
\mathcal D=\lambda\partial_m
\quad\text{or}\quad
\mathcal D=\Delta\partial_m.
\]

Use safe depth

\[
R\asymp \frac{\log Q}{\log\log Q}.
\]

The basis-normalized Wronskian rows should be modeled as

\[
G_0=Z_\alpha,
\]

\[
G_{j+1}=\phi_j(\mathcal D)q''_{\can},
\qquad 0\le j\le R,
\]

where

\[
\phi_0,\ldots,\phi_{R+1}
\]

are a stable basis on the active frequency nodes.

The determinant matrix is

\[
M_{j\ell}(m,\alpha)
=
\phi_\ell(\mathcal D)G_j(m,\alpha),
\qquad
0\le j,\ell\le R+1,
\]

and

\[
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R(m,\alpha)=\det M(m,\alpha).
\]

Use row-normalized determinant size:

\[
\eta_W
=
\frac{|\det M|}
{\prod_{j=0}^{R+1}\|M_{j,\bullet}\|_2}.
\]

This is essential. Raw determinant size alone is not interpretable.

---

# 2. Track L — Low-prime plateau block stress test

Use blocks

\[
\mathcal B_{a,b}(Q)
=
\{p:Q^a\le p\le Q^b\},
\]

with

\[
0<a<b
\]

fixed or slowly varying.

Then

\[
u_p=\log p\in[a\log Q,b\log Q],
\]

\[
u_0=\frac{a+b}{2}\log Q,
\]

\[
L=(b-a)\log Q.
\]

Normalize nodes by

\[
x_p=\frac{2(u_p-u_0)}{L}\in[-1,1].
\]

The key diagnostic for low-prime blocks is whether

\[
x_p
\]

has enough spread and enough prime nodes to support rank \(R+2\):

\[
|\mathcal B_{a,b}|\gg R+2.
\]

For each block, compute:

\[
c_p^+
\sim
\frac{\Lambda(p)}{\sqrt p}
V_q^+(p/N_q)
(\log p)^3,
\]

\[
z_p^+(\alpha)
\sim
\frac{\Lambda(p)}{\sqrt p}
V_Z^+(p/N_Z)
p^{-\alpha},
\]

and the ratio

\[
r_p^+(\alpha)=\frac{z_p^+(\alpha)}{c_p^+}.
\]

For a first exact-like proxy:

\[
r_p^+(\alpha)
=
p^{-\alpha}(\log p)^{-3}A_\alpha^+(u_p).
\]

Then compute the normalized ratio

\[
\widehat r_p^+(\alpha)
=
\frac{r_p^+(\alpha)}{r_{p_0}^+(\alpha)}.
\]

The low-prime plateau rank test is:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank,L}
=
\inf_{P\in\mathcal P_{\le R}}
\max_{p\in\mathcal B_{a,b}}
|\widehat r_p^+(\alpha)-P(x_p)|.
\]

Also compute intrinsic variation:

\[
\mathcal V_{\alpha,L}
=
L\left(
|\alpha|+u_0^{-1}
+
\|\partial_u\log A_\alpha^+\|_{L^\infty(\mathcal B)}
\right).
\]

A low-prime block is admissible for stress testing only if:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank,L}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}
\]

and

\[
\mathcal V_{\alpha,L}\ge R^{-C_0}.
\]

If these fail, any determinant collapse is rank-degenerate and should be ignored.

---

# 3. Track C — Conductor-scale / saddle block stress test

Use blocks centered near the natural AFE support:

\[
n\asymp N_q(m)
\quad\text{and/or}\quad
n\asymp N_Z(m,\alpha).
\]

Let

\[
\mathcal B_C
=
\{n:\log n\in[u_0-L/2,u_0+L/2]\},
\]

where \(u_0\) is chosen around the main AFE saddle.

For prime-only first pass:

\[
n=p.
\]

For fuller AFE pass, include prime powers or all \(n\) with von Mangoldt weights.

Define nodes:

\[
x_n^+
=
\frac{2(\log n-u_0^+)}{L^+},
\]

and for dual branch:

\[
x_n^-
=
\frac{2(\log n-u_0^-)}{L^-}.
\]

Compute branch ratios:

\[
r_n^+(\alpha)=\frac{z_n^+(\alpha)}{c_n^+},
\qquad
r_n^-(\alpha)=\frac{z_n^-(\alpha)}{c_n^-}.
\]

The two-branch symbol-rank diagnostic is:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank,C}
=
\inf_{P\in\mathcal P_{\le R}}
\max
\left(
\max_{n\in\mathcal B_+}|r_n^+(\alpha)-P(x_n^+)|,
\max_{n\in\mathcal B_-}|r_n^-(\alpha)-P(x_n^-)|
\right).
\]

This must be monitored separately from determinant size.

A conductor-scale collapse is meaningful only if:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank,C}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}.
\]

---

# 4. Basis construction and conditioning diagnostic

For each active block, build one of:

1. **Discrete orthonormal basis** on the actual nodes \(x_n\) with weights \(|c_n|^2\).
2. **Chebyshev basis** \(T_j(x)\), \(0\le j\le R+1\).
3. **Stabilized Lagrange basis** on selected interpolation nodes.

Preferred first implementation:

\[
\text{discrete orthonormal basis}.
\]

Let

\[
\Phi_{nj}=\phi_j(x_n).
\]

Compute basis conditioning:

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}
=
\kappa(\Phi^\ast W\Phi),
\]

or, after orthonormalization,

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}
=
\frac{\sigma_{\max}(\Phi)}
{\sigma_{\min}(\Phi)}.
\]

Accept only blocks with:

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}\le Q^{C_\kappa}.
\]

If

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}\gg Q^{C_\kappa},
\]

small \(\eta_W\) is likely a basis artifact.

---

# 5. Determinant diagnostics

For each candidate \((m,\alpha,\mathcal B)\), compute:

## 5.1 Zero smallness

\[
\eta_Z
=
\frac{|Z_\alpha(m)|}
{\left(\sum_{\nu\in\mathcal B}|z_\nu(\alpha)|^2\right)^{1/2}}.
\]

## 5.2 Wronskian smallness

\[
\eta_W
=
\frac{|\det M|}
{\prod_{j=0}^{R+1}\|M_{j,\bullet}\|_2}.
\]

## 5.3 Singular-value profile

Compute

\[
\sigma_1(M)\ge\cdots\ge\sigma_{R+2}(M).
\]

Record

\[
\eta_{\rm sv}
=
\frac{\sigma_{R+2}(M)}{\sigma_1(M)}.
\]

This distinguishes determinant collapse by one near-linear dependency from uniform row shrinkage.

## 5.4 Symbol rank

Use \(\rho_{\rm rank,L}\) for low-prime blocks and \(\rho_{\rm rank,C}\) for conductor blocks.

## 5.5 Intrinsic variation

\[
\mathcal V_\alpha
=
L\left(
|\alpha|+u_0^{-1}
+
\|\partial_u\log A_\alpha\|_\infty
\right).
\]

## 5.6 \(E_2\) tolerance margin

Let the assumed canonical derivative error be:

\[
\max_{0\le k\le2R+1}|\mathcal D^k E_2|
\le \eta_{E_2}.
\]

Define a determinant error margin:

\[
\eta_{E_2}^{\det}
\asymp
C_R Q^{C(R+1)}\eta_{E_2}
\]

or, better, compute it by perturbing rows:

\[
M\mapsto M+\delta M,
\qquad
\|\delta M_{j,\bullet}\|_2\le \eta_{E_2}.
\]

A collapse is serious only if:

\[
\eta_W \ll \eta_{E_2}^{\det}.
\]

Actually, more precisely: if the observed determinant is **not** separated from the \(E_2\) perturbation scale, it cannot be interpreted as actual. A dangerous counterexample requires smallness that survives the allowed \(E_2\)-perturbation model.

---

# 6. Main+dual folded branch model

Use two branches:

\[
G_0^{\rm md}(m,\alpha)
=
\sum_{\nu\in\mathcal B_+}
z_\nu^+(\alpha)e^{-im u_\nu^+}
+
\sum_{\nu\in\mathcal B_-}
z_\nu^-(\alpha)e^{+im u_\nu^-}
+
B_Z(m,\alpha),
\]

and

\[
G_{j+1}^{\rm md}(m)
=
\sum_{\nu\in\mathcal B_+}
c_\nu^+\phi_j(x_\nu^+)e^{-im u_\nu^+}
+
\sum_{\nu\in\mathcal B_-}
c_\nu^-\phi_j(x_\nu^-)e^{+im u_\nu^-}
+
B_{j+1}(m).
\]

Then

\[
M_{j\ell}^{\rm md}
=
\phi_\ell(\mathcal D)G_j^{\rm md}.
\]

Run three models:

1. **Main-only model**
   \[
   D_2=0.
   \]

2. **Formal mirrored dual**
   \[
   c_n^-\approx \overline{c_n^+},
   \qquad
   z_n^-\approx \overline{z_n^+}.
   \]

3. **Actual-like folded dual**
   using gamma-weighted branch amplitudes and saddle-dependent weights.

Only model 3 is meaningful for stop-rule purposes.

---

# 7. Search procedure

## Stage 1 — Rank and conditioning scan

For each \(R\), block type, and \(\alpha\):

1. compute \(\rho_{\rm rank}\);
2. compute \(\mathcal V_\alpha\);
3. compute \(\kappa_{\rm basis}\).

Reject block if:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}<Q^{-C_{\rm rank}},
\]

or

\[
\mathcal V_\alpha<R^{-C_0},
\]

or

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}>Q^{C_\kappa}.
\]

## Stage 2 — Phase-relaxed negative control

Replace

\[
e^{-im\log n}
\]

by independent phases

\[
e^{-i\theta_n}.
\]

Minimize:

\[
\mathcal J_{\rm rel}
=
\eta_Z^2+\mu\eta_W^2.
\]

Expected: collapse should be found.

If phase-relaxed collapse is **not** found, the implementation is likely too rigid or incorrectly normalized.

## Stage 3 — One-parameter orbit search

Now impose:

\[
\theta_n=m\log n.
\]

Minimize:

\[
\mathcal J_{\rm orb}
=
\eta_Z^2+\mu\eta_W^2
+
\nu\max(0,Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}-\rho_{\rm rank})^2
+
\omega\max(0,\kappa_{\rm basis}-Q^{C_\kappa})^2.
\]

Search over:

\[
m,\quad \alpha,\quad \mathcal B.
\]

Use coarse scan plus local refinement.

## Stage 4 — Add \(B_2\)

Include background terms and rerun Stage 3.

If collapse disappears only after \(B_2\), test whether disappearance is stable or merely shifts the target.

## Stage 5 — Add main+dual folded branches

Run main-only, formal-dual, then actual-like dual.

The most important comparison is:

\[
\eta_W^{+}
\quad\text{vs}\quad
\eta_W^{+/-}.
\]

If dual kills collapse robustly, the incidence theorem may be exploiting true main/dual compatibility.

If dual merely shifts collapse, the theorem remains hard.

## Stage 6 — \(E_2\)-tolerance perturbation

Perturb rows within the allowed derivative error budget.

A candidate survives only if determinant smallness remains after worst-case or random \(E_2\)-consistent perturbations.

---

# 8. Collapse classification

## Class 0 — Degenerate rank collapse

Criteria:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}<Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}
\]

or

\[
\mathcal V_\alpha<R^{-C_0}.
\]

Interpretation:

\[
\boxed{\text{Ignore. }Z_\alpha\text{ did not add usable rank.}}
\]

## Class 1 — Basis-conditioning collapse

Criteria:

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}>Q^{C_\kappa}
\]

or determinant smallness disappears after orthonormalization.

Interpretation:

\[
\boxed{\text{Ignore. Coordinate artifact.}}
\]

## Class 2 — Phase-relaxed-only collapse

Criteria:

Collapse occurs for independent phases but not for the one-parameter orbit.

Interpretation:

\[
\boxed{\text{Expected negative control. Not a stop rule.}}
\]

## Class 3 — One-parameter orbit collapse

Criteria:

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z},
\qquad
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W},
\]

with healthy rank and conditioning, on the actual prime-log orbit.

Interpretation:

\[
\boxed{\text{Yellow warning. Determinant nonrecurrence is genuinely hard.}}
\]

## Class 4 — Actual-like main+dual collapse

Criteria:

Class 3 persists after:

\[
B_2,
\qquad
\text{actual-like }D_2,
\qquad
E_2\text{ tolerance}.
\]

Interpretation:

\[
\boxed{\text{Red warning. Pause Wronskian proof sprint if persistent in }R.}
\]

---

# 9. Stop-rule criteria

Pause the Wronskian proof route if a sequence of candidates with increasing \(R\) satisfies:

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z},
\]

\[
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W},
\]

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}},
\]

\[
\mathcal V_\alpha\ge R^{-C_0},
\]

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}\le Q^{C_\kappa},
\]

and survives:

\[
+B_2,
\qquad
+\text{actual-like }D_2,
\qquad
+E_2\text{ perturbation}.
\]

A single low-\(R\) event is not enough. The red pattern must improve or persist as \(R\) increases.

Continue the Wronskian route if collapses are only:

\[
\text{rank-degenerate},
\]

\[
\text{basis-conditioned},
\]

\[
\text{phase-relaxed only},
\]

or killed robustly by actual-like main+dual coupling.

---

# 10. Minimal run table

Each candidate should be logged as:

\[
(R,Q,\text{track},a,b,u_0,L,|\mathcal B|,\alpha,m)
\]

with:

\[
\eta_Z,\quad
\eta_W,\quad
\rho_{\rm rank},\quad
\mathcal V_\alpha,\quad
\kappa_{\rm basis},\quad
\eta_{\rm sv},\quad
\eta_{E_2}^{\det}.
\]

Also record model level:

\[
\text{phase-relaxed},
\quad
\text{orbit},
\quad
+B_2,
\quad
+\text{formal dual},
\quad
+\text{actual-like dual},
\quad
+E_2.
\]

---

# 11. Final diagnosis

This stress test is designed to answer one question:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Can the actual-like prime-log orbit make }
Z_\alpha\approx0
\text{ and }
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R\approx0
\text{ despite healthy symbol rank?}
}
\]

If yes, the Wronskian route needs a much stronger arithmetic theorem than currently identified.

If no, the route becomes substantially more plausible, because the remaining obstruction would no longer appear in the strongest actual-like surrogate.

_Thought for 7s_

## Agent 5 / E — Packet-frequency determinant stress-test design

Instruction applied: refine the determinant stress test so it uses **packet-frequency / conductor-scale active blocks**, not low-prime plateau blocks, with target

\[
Z_\alpha(m)\approx0,
\qquad
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R(m,\alpha)\approx0
\]

under healthy symbol rank and conditioning. 

The key correction from the latest coordination note is that low-prime plateau blocks are useful only as symbol-rank sanity checks. They are not the right determinant blocks because, at ordinary packet scale \(\lambda Q\asymp1\), they give compressed nodes \(x_p=\lambda\log p\asymp(\log Q)/Q\ll1\). The determinant stress test must instead use frequencies satisfying \(\lambda\log n\asymp1\). 

---

# 1. Active block definition

Use packet-frequency blocks

\[
\mathcal B_{\mathrm{pf}}
=
\{n: u_- \le \log n\le u_+\},
\]

where

\[
u_0=\frac{u_-+u_+}{2},
\qquad
L=u_+-u_-,
\]

and impose

\[
\boxed{
\lambda u_0\asymp1,
\qquad
\lambda L\gtrsim R^{-C_0}.
}
\]

Equivalently, for ordinary scale

\[
\lambda\asymp Q^{-1},
\]

use

\[
\boxed{
\log n\asymp Q.
}
\]

Normalize nodes by

\[
x_n=\frac{2(\lambda\log n-\lambda u_0)}{\lambda L}
=
\frac{2(\log n-u_0)}{L}
\in[-1,1].
\]

This removes the artificial node compression that kills the low-prime determinant.

Minimum block requirements:

\[
|\mathcal B_{\mathrm{pf}}|\ge c(R+2)
\]

with \(c\ge4\) preferred for numerical stability, and all relevant weights must satisfy

\[
|c_n|\ge Q^{-C_{\mathrm{amp}}}
\cdot
\max_{\nu\in\mathcal B_{\mathrm{pf}}}|c_\nu|
\]

after amplitude normalization.

---

# 2. Two stress tracks

## Track C1 — Main packet-frequency block

Use the main AFE branch

\[
q''_{\can,+}(m)
\approx
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_+}
c_n^+ e^{-im\log n}.
\]

The packet-frequency condition is

\[
\lambda\log n\asymp1.
\]

Set

\[
x_n^+=\frac{2(\lambda\log n-\lambda u_0^+)}{\lambda L^+}.
\]

Use this track to test whether the main branch alone can produce determinant collapse.

## Track C2 — Full folded main+dual block

Use

\[
q''_{\can}^{\mathrm{md}}(m)
\approx
B_2(m)
+
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_+}
c_n^+ e^{-im\log n}
+
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_-}
c_n^- e^{+im\log n}.
\]

Use dual normalized nodes

\[
x_n^-=
\frac{2(\lambda\log n-\lambda u_0^-)}{\lambda L^-}
\]

or, if the chosen convention folds around the saddle,

\[
x_n^-=
\frac{2\left(\lambda\log\frac{m}{2\pi n}-\lambda v_0^-\right)}
{\lambda L^-}.
\]

Track C2 is the decisive track. A collapse in C1 but not C2 suggests genuine main/dual obstruction.

---

# 3. Basis-normalized Wronskian object

Choose a stable basis

\[
\phi_0,\ldots,\phi_{R+1}
\]

on the normalized packet-frequency nodes. Preferred implementation:

\[
\text{discrete orthonormal basis with weights } |c_n|^2.
\]

Define rows

\[
G_0=Z_\alpha,
\]

\[
G_{j+1}=\phi_j(\mathcal D)q''_{\can},
\qquad 0\le j\le R.
\]

Define columns by applying the same stable basis operators:

\[
M_{j\ell}(m,\alpha)
=
\phi_\ell(\mathcal D)G_j(m,\alpha),
\qquad
0\le j,\ell\le R+1.
\]

Then

\[
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R(m,\alpha)=\det M(m,\alpha).
\]

For a single branch, the approximate entries are:

\[
M_{0\ell}
=
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B}
z_n(\alpha)\phi_\ell(x_n)e^{-im\log n},
\]

\[
M_{j+1,\ell}
=
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B}
c_n\phi_j(x_n)\phi_\ell(x_n)e^{-im\log n}.
\]

For the folded main+dual model:

\[
M_{0\ell}^{\mathrm{md}}
=
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_+}
z_n^+(\alpha)\phi_\ell(x_n^+)e^{-im\log n}
+
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_-}
z_n^-(\alpha)\phi_\ell(x_n^-)e^{+im\log n}
+
B_{0\ell},
\]

\[
M_{j+1,\ell}^{\mathrm{md}}
=
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_+}
c_n^+\phi_j(x_n^+)\phi_\ell(x_n^+)e^{-im\log n}
+
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_-}
c_n^-\phi_j(x_n^-)\phi_\ell(x_n^-)e^{+im\log n}
+
B_{j+1,\ell}.
\]

---

# 4. Diagnostics

## 4.1 Zero smallness

\[
\eta_Z
=
\frac{|Z_\alpha(m)|}
{\left(
\sum_{\pm}\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_\pm}
|z_n^\pm(\alpha)|^2
\right)^{1/2}}.
\]

Danger threshold:

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z}.
\]

## 4.2 Wronskian smallness

Use row-normalized determinant size:

\[
\eta_W
=
\frac{|\det M|}
{\prod_{j=0}^{R+1}\|M_{j,\bullet}\|_2}.
\]

Danger threshold:

\[
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W}.
\]

Also record singular values:

\[
\sigma_1(M)\ge\cdots\ge\sigma_{R+2}(M),
\]

and

\[
\eta_{\mathrm{sv}}
=
\frac{\sigma_{R+2}(M)}{\sigma_1(M)}.
\]

This distinguishes genuine row dependence from simple amplitude collapse.

## 4.3 Symbol rank

For the full two-branch test, compute

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}
=
\inf_{P\in\mathcal P_{\le R}}
\max_{\pm}
\max_{n\in\mathcal B_\pm}
|r_n^\pm(\alpha,m)-P(x_n^\pm)|.
\]

where

\[
r_n^\pm(\alpha,m)=\frac{z_n^\pm(\alpha,m)}{c_n^\pm(m)}.
\]

Healthy rank threshold:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}.
\]

If this fails, any determinant collapse is classified as symbol-rank-degenerate.

## 4.4 Basis conditioning

Let

\[
\Phi_{\nu j}=\phi_j(x_\nu)
\]

on the active branch nodes, with weights \(w_\nu=|c_\nu|^2\). Compute

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}
=
\kappa(\Phi^\ast W\Phi).
\]

If discrete orthonormalization is exact, monitor numerical singular values of the basis evaluation matrix.

Healthy conditioning threshold:

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}\le Q^{C_\kappa}.
\]

## 4.5 Node-spread admissibility

Record

\[
\operatorname{diam}(x)
=
\max x_\nu-\min x_\nu.
\]

Require

\[
\operatorname{diam}(x)\gtrsim R^{-C_x}.
\]

This explicitly prevents accidental reversion to the low-prime compressed-node failure.

## 4.6 \(E_2\)-tolerance margin

Assume a canonical derivative error budget

\[
\max_{0\le k\le2R+1}|\mathcal D^kE_2|
\le \eta_{E_2}.
\]

Propagate this to determinant scale by row perturbation:

\[
M\mapsto M+\delta M,
\qquad
\|\delta M_{j,\bullet}\|_2\le \eta_{E_2,j}.
\]

Define

\[
\eta_{E_2}^{\det}
=
\sup_{\delta M}
\frac{|\det(M+\delta M)-\det M|}
{\prod_j\|M_{j,\bullet}\|_2}.
\]

A collapse is meaningful only if it remains small after admissible \(E_2\)-perturbation.

---

# 5. Staged models

## Stage 0 — Block admissibility

For each candidate block, check:

\[
\lambda u_0\asymp1,
\]

\[
\lambda L\gtrsim R^{-C_0},
\]

\[
|\mathcal B|\gg R,
\]

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}\le Q^{C_\kappa},
\]

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}.
\]

Reject blocks failing these before any determinant search.

## Stage 1 — Phase-relaxed negative control

Replace

\[
e^{-im\log n}
\]

by independent phases

\[
e^{-i\theta_n}.
\]

Minimize

\[
\mathcal J_{\rm rel}
=
\eta_Z^2+\mu\eta_W^2
+
\nu\max(0,Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}-\rho_{\rm rank})^2
+
\omega\max(0,\kappa_{\rm basis}-Q^{C_\kappa})^2.
\]

Expected: collapse should occur.

If no phase-relaxed collapse occurs, the implementation may be too rigid, the block too small, or the determinant too heavily normalized.

Classification:

\[
\boxed{\text{phase-relaxed collapse is a negative control, not a stop rule.}}
\]

## Stage 2 — One-parameter orbit

Impose

\[
\theta_n=m\log n.
\]

Search over

\[
m,\quad\alpha,\quad \mathcal B_{\mathrm{pf}}.
\]

Minimize

\[
\mathcal J_{\rm orb}
=
\eta_Z^2+\mu\eta_W^2
+
\nu\max(0,Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}-\rho_{\rm rank})^2
+
\omega\max(0,\kappa_{\rm basis}-Q^{C_\kappa})^2.
\]

Use:

1. coarse scan over \(m\);
2. local refinement in \((m,\alpha)\);
3. block mutation: vary \(u_0,L\);
4. branch mutation: main-only vs folded dual.

A successful Stage 2 collapse is a Yellow warning.

## Stage 3 — Add \(B_2\)

Use the canonical background

\[
B_2(t)
=
-\frac18\Re\psi^{(2)}
\!\left(\frac14+\frac{it}{2}\right).
\]

Include

\[
\phi_\ell(\mathcal D)B_2
\]

in each relevant row/column entry.

Re-run Stage 2.

Interpretation:

- Collapse survives \(B_2\): background is not the obstruction.
- Collapse disappears but nearby shifted collapse appears: \(B_2\) is only moving the target.
- Collapse robustly disappears across blocks: possible background-assisted incidence.

## Stage 4 — Add formal dual

Add a mirrored dual branch with simplified coefficients:

\[
c_n^-\approx \overline{c_n^+},
\qquad
z_n^-\approx \overline{z_n^+}.
\]

This tests whether two-branch geometry alone blocks collapse.

Classification:

\[
\text{collapse killed by formal dual}
\Rightarrow
\text{main/dual geometry may matter.}
\]

But this is still not a stop-rule green signal.

## Stage 5 — Add actual-like folded dual

Use gamma-weighted folded branch symbols:

\[
c_n^-(m),
\qquad
z_n^-(\alpha,m),
\]

with saddle-dependent amplitudes and exact or exact-like normalized nodes.

Re-run the search.

This is the first serious model for the canonical Wronskian.

## Stage 6 — \(E_2\)-tolerance perturbation

Perturb all rows within the high-derivative AFE error budget.

Accept a collapse only if

\[
\eta_W
\]

remains below threshold after admissible perturbation or if the determinant smallness is far below

\[
\eta_{E_2}^{\det}.
\]

---

# 6. Search parameters

Use safe depth progression:

\[
R\in\{4,6,8,10,12,16,20,24\}.
\]

For each \(R\), set

\[
Q
\]

large enough that

\[
R\approx c\frac{\log Q}{\log\log Q}.
\]

Packet-frequency block parameters:

\[
\lambda u_0\in[0.5,2],
\]

\[
\lambda L\in
\{R^{-2},R^{-1},R^{-1/2},1\}.
\]

Reject blocks where

\[
\lambda L<R^{-C_0}.
\]

Use block cardinalities:

\[
|\mathcal B|\in
\{2(R+2),4(R+2),8(R+2)\}.
\]

Alpha search:

\[
\alpha L\in
\{0,\pm R^{-2},\pm R^{-1},\pm1,\pm2\}.
\]

Since \(\alpha\to0\) is a degeneration risk, report \(\alpha=0\) separately rather than mixing it into generic statistics.

---

# 7. Collapse classification

## Class 0 — Node-compression collapse

Criteria:

\[
\operatorname{diam}(x)\ll R^{-C_x}.
\]

Interpretation:

\[
\boxed{\text{Ignore. This is the low-prime failure mode.}}
\]

## Class 1 — Symbol-rank-degenerate collapse

Criteria:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}<Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}.
\]

Interpretation:

\[
\boxed{\text{Ignore. }Z_\alpha\text{ did not add usable rank.}}
\]

## Class 2 — Basis-conditioning collapse

Criteria:

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}>Q^{C_\kappa}
\]

or collapse vanishes after orthonormalization.

Interpretation:

\[
\boxed{\text{Ignore. Coordinate artifact.}}
\]

## Class 3 — Phase-relaxed-only collapse

Collapse occurs with independent phases but not on the one-parameter orbit.

Interpretation:

\[
\boxed{\text{Expected; not a stop rule.}}
\]

## Class 4 — One-parameter packet-frequency collapse

Criteria:

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z},
\]

\[
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W},
\]

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}},
\]

\[
\operatorname{diam}(x)\gtrsim R^{-C_x},
\]

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}\le Q^{C_\kappa}.
\]

Interpretation:

\[
\boxed{\text{Yellow warning. Incidence theorem is genuinely hard.}}
\]

## Class 5 — Actual-like main+dual collapse

Class 4 survives:

\[
+B_2,
\]

\[
+\text{actual-like }D_2,
\]

\[
+E_2\text{ tolerance}.
\]

Interpretation:

\[
\boxed{\text{Red warning if persistent with increasing }R.}
}
\]

---

# 8. Stop-rule criteria

Pause the Wronskian proof route if there is a persistent sequence, as \(R\) increases, satisfying:

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z},
\]

\[
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W},
\]

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}},
\]

\[
\operatorname{diam}(x)\gtrsim R^{-C_x},
\]

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}\le Q^{C_\kappa},
\]

and the collapse survives:

\[
B_2+D_2+E_2.
\]

A single small-\(R\) collapse is not sufficient. The pattern must persist or strengthen with growing \(R\).

Continue the Wronskian route if collapses are only:

\[
\text{node-compressed},
\]

\[
\text{rank-degenerate},
\]

\[
\text{basis-conditioned},
\]

\[
\text{phase-relaxed only},
\]

or killed robustly by actual-like main/dual coupling.

---

# 9. Minimal reporting table

Each candidate should report:

\[
R,\ Q,\ \lambda,\ u_0,\ L,\ |\mathcal B|,
\ \alpha,\ m,
\]

\[
\lambda u_0,\quad
\lambda L,\quad
\operatorname{diam}(x),
\]

\[
\eta_Z,\quad
\eta_W,\quad
\eta_{\rm sv},\quad
\rho_{\rm rank},\quad
\kappa_{\rm basis},\quad
\eta_{E_2}^{\det},
\]

and model level:

\[
\text{relaxed},
\quad
\text{orbit},
\quad
+B_2,
\quad
+\text{formal dual},
\quad
+\text{actual-like dual},
\quad
+E_2.
\]

---

# 10. Final stress-test target

The refined stress test is designed to decide whether the actual-like packet-frequency orbit can realize:

\[
Z_\alpha(m)\approx0,
\]

\[
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R(m,\alpha)\approx0,
\]

while preserving:

\[
\lambda\log n\asymp1,
\]

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}},
\]

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}\le Q^{C_\kappa},
\]

and canonical tolerance under:

\[
B_2+D_2+E_2.
\]

If yes, the Wronskian route likely requires a deeper arithmetic incidence theorem than currently formulated.

If no, the Wronskian route remains viable for ordinary-scale Gate 1.

## Agent 5 / E — Adversarial packet-frequency stress test

Instruction applied: adversarially test the packet-frequency Wronskian route using blocks

\[
\mathcal B_{\rm pf}
=
\{n:cQ\le\log n\le cQ+Q/R\},
\qquad 0<c<1/2,
\]

with

\[
x_n=\lambda\log n\asymp1,
\qquad
\operatorname{diam}(x)\asymp R^{-1}.
\]

The target is to find whether one can force

\[
Z_\alpha(m)\approx0,
\qquad
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R(m,\alpha)\approx0
\]

while maintaining healthy symbol rank, healthy basis conditioning, and determinant-admissible node spread. 

---

# 1. Verdict

The packet-frequency correction removes the previous low-prime node-collapse objection, but it does **not** remove the adversarial mechanism.

I expect the following ladder behavior:

\[
\boxed{
\text{phase-relaxed collapse: yes}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{one-parameter orbit collapse: plausible / must be tested}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
B_2\text{ should not kill collapse by itself}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{formal dual may or may not kill collapse}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{actual-like dual is the first serious obstruction}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
E_2\text{ is only a tolerance filter, not an anti-collapse mechanism}
}
\]

No stop-rule counterexample is established analytically here. But a realistic stress protocol can now be made sharp enough that a persistent collapse would be meaningful.

---

# 2. Packet-frequency adversarial model

Use an active block

\[
\mathcal B=\{n:u_-\le\log n\le u_+\},
\]

with

\[
u_0\asymp Q,
\qquad
L=u_+-u_-\asymp Q/R.
\]

Since

\[
\lambda Q\asymp1,
\]

we have

\[
\lambda u_0\asymp1,
\qquad
\lambda L\asymp R^{-1}.
\]

Normalize nodes by

\[
x_n=\frac{2(\log n-u_0)}{L}\in[-1,1].
\]

This guarantees

\[
\operatorname{diam}(x)\asymp1
\]

in the affine-normalized basis variable, and

\[
\operatorname{diam}(\lambda\log n)\asymp R^{-1}
\]

in raw normalized derivative frequency. Both are acceptable for a basis-normalized Wronskian at depth \(R\), provided the basis is stable.

Use rows

\[
G_0=Z_\alpha,
\]

\[
G_{j+1}=\phi_j(\mathcal D)q''_{\can},
\qquad 0\le j\le R,
\]

where \(\phi_j\) is a stable basis on \(x_n\). Define

\[
M_{j\ell}
=
\phi_\ell(\mathcal D)G_j,
\qquad
0\le j,\ell\le R+1,
\]

and

\[
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R=\det M.
\]

For the main-only packet-frequency surrogate,

\[
M_{0\ell}
=
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B}
z_n^+(\alpha)\phi_\ell(x_n)e^{-im\log n},
\]

\[
M_{j+1,\ell}
=
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B}
c_n^+\phi_j(x_n)\phi_\ell(x_n)e^{-im\log n}.
\]

For main+dual,

\[
M_{0\ell}^{\pm}
=
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_+}
z_n^+(\alpha)\phi_\ell(x_n^+)e^{-im\log n}
+
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_-}
z_n^-(\alpha)\phi_\ell(x_n^-)e^{+im\log n}
+
B_{0\ell},
\]

\[
M_{j+1,\ell}^{\pm}
=
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_+}
c_n^+\phi_j(x_n^+)\phi_\ell(x_n^+)e^{-im\log n}
+
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_-}
c_n^-\phi_j(x_n^-)\phi_\ell(x_n^-)e^{+im\log n}
+
B_{j+1,\ell}.
\]

---

# 3. Required healthy diagnostics

A collapse is admissible only if all three structural diagnostics pass.

## 3.1 Symbol-rank residual

Compute

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}
=
\inf_{P\in\mathcal P_{\le R}}
\max_{\pm}\max_{n\in\mathcal B_\pm}
|r_n^\pm(\alpha,m)-P(x_n^\pm)|.
\]

Require

\[
\boxed{
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}.
}
\]

If this fails, \(Z_\alpha\) is symbolically redundant with the \(q''_{\can}\)-jet rows, so determinant collapse is not meaningful.

## 3.2 Basis conditioning

Let

\[
\Phi_{nj}=\phi_j(x_n)
\]

with weights

\[
w_n=|c_n|^2.
\]

Require

\[
\boxed{
\kappa_{\rm basis}
=
\kappa(\Phi^\ast W\Phi)
\le Q^{C_\kappa}.
}
\]

If using discrete orthonormal basis, monitor numerical singular values of the evaluation matrix instead.

## 3.3 Node spread

Require

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{diam}(\lambda\log n)\gtrsim R^{-C_x}
}
\]

or equivalently, after affine normalization,

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{diam}(x)\asymp1.
}
\]

This rejects the old low-prime failure mode.

---

# 4. Collapse objective

Use normalized zero smallness

\[
\eta_Z
=
\frac{|Z_\alpha(m)|}
{\left(
\sum_{\pm}\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_\pm}
|z_n^\pm(\alpha)|^2
\right)^{1/2}},
\]

and row-normalized determinant smallness

\[
\eta_W
=
\frac{|\det M|}
{\prod_{j=0}^{R+1}\|M_{j,\bullet}\|_2}.
\]

A candidate collapse satisfies

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z},
\qquad
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W},
\]

with

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}},
\qquad
\kappa_{\rm basis}\le Q^{C_\kappa},
\qquad
\operatorname{diam}(\lambda\log n)\gtrsim R^{-C_x}.
\]

Also record

\[
\eta_{\rm sv}
=
\frac{\sigma_{R+2}(M)}{\sigma_1(M)}
\]

to distinguish determinant collapse by one nearly dependent direction from amplitude shrinkage.

---

# 5. Stage A — Phase-relaxed model

Replace

\[
e^{-im\log n}
\]

by independent phases

\[
u_n=e^{-i\theta_n}.
\]

The system becomes a finite torus model:

\[
Z_\alpha(u)=0,
\qquad
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R(u,\alpha)=0.
\]

The variables are the phases \(\theta_n\) plus \(\alpha\). The constraints are two complex scalar equations if we target only \(Z_\alpha\) and \(\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R\), or more if we also force small finite jets.

Since

\[
|\mathcal B|\gg R,
\]

the phase-relaxed model has far more degrees of freedom than constraints. I expect collapse generically.

Minimize

\[
\mathcal J_{\rm rel}
=
\eta_Z^2+\mu\eta_W^2
+
\nu\max(0,Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}-\rho_{\rm rank})^2
+
\omega\max(0,\kappa_{\rm basis}-Q^{C_\kappa})^2.
\]

Expected result:

\[
\boxed{
\text{phase-relaxed collapse occurs.}
}
\]

Interpretation:

This is a negative control. It confirms that determinant rank and packet-frequency node spread do not by themselves forbid collapse.

---

# 6. Stage B — One-parameter prime-log orbit

Now impose

\[
u_n=e^{-im\log n}.
\]

Search over

\[
m,\quad \alpha,\quad c,\quad L,\quad \mathcal B.
\]

Use

\[
\mathcal B=
\{n:cQ\le\log n\le cQ+Q/R\}.
\]

Minimize

\[
\mathcal J_{\rm orb}
=
\eta_Z^2+\mu\eta_W^2
+
\nu\max(0,Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}-\rho_{\rm rank})^2
+
\omega\max(0,\kappa_{\rm basis}-Q^{C_\kappa})^2.
\]

A one-parameter collapse is more serious because it means the prime-log orbit can approach the bad determinant incidence set.

Heuristic recurrence cost:

The target event

\[
Z_\alpha\approx0,
\qquad
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R\approx0
\]

is roughly codimension \(4\) real if only these two complex quantities are constrained. If one instead forces the whole finite-jet bad tube,

\[
Z_\alpha\approx0,
\qquad
\mathcal D^j q''_{\can}\approx0,\quad0\le j\le R,
\]

then the codimension is roughly

\[
2(R+2).
\]

The Wronskian incidence search is therefore potentially easier to hit than the full bad-jet event. A collapse here does not automatically disprove Gate 1, but it attacks the Wronskian proof mechanism.

Interpretation:

\[
\boxed{
\text{one-parameter collapse = Yellow warning.}
}
\]

---

# 7. Stage C — Add \(B_2\)

Include canonical background

\[
B_2(t)
=
-\frac18\Re\psi^{(2)}
\left(\frac14+\frac{it}{2}\right).
\]

In the stress model this contributes deterministic row/column shifts

\[
B_{j\ell}(m)=\phi_\ell(\mathcal D)\phi_j(\mathcal D)B_2(m),
\]

for rows derived from \(q''_{\can}\). \(B_2\) does not affect \(Z_\alpha\) unless the chosen \(Z_\alpha\)-definition includes corresponding completed gamma terms.

Expected behavior:

\[
\boxed{
B_2\text{ shifts the target but should not kill collapse generically.}
}
\]

A strong result would be if collapse disappears robustly across blocks and does not reappear after changing \(m,\alpha,c,L\). That would suggest background-assisted incidence, but I regard that as unlikely.

---

# 8. Stage D — Add formal dual

Add a mirrored dual branch:

\[
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_-}c_n^-e^{+im\log n},
\qquad
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_-}z_n^-(\alpha)e^{+im\log n}.
\]

First use a formal model:

\[
c_n^-\approx \overline{c_n^+},
\qquad
z_n^-\approx \overline{z_n^+}.
\]

Run both:

\[
x_n^-=x_n^+
\]

and a folded saddle coordinate

\[
x_n^-=\lambda\log\frac{m}{2\pi n}
\]

depending on the exact AFE convention.

Possible outcomes:

1. **Collapse persists.**  
   Formal duality alone does not help.

2. **Collapse dies only when \(x^+\approx x^-\).**  
   Saddle overlap may create rank degeneration or extra structure.

3. **Collapse dies robustly.**  
   Two-branch geometry may matter.

This stage is still not actual-zeta decisive.

---

# 9. Stage E — Add actual-like dual

Use gamma-weighted dual amplitudes and saddle-dependent weights:

\[
c_n^-(m),
\qquad
z_n^-(\alpha,m),
\]

with exact-like AFE weight ratios. This is the first serious approximation to the canonical determinant.

Recompute

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}
=
\inf_{P\in\mathcal P_{\le R}}
\max_{\pm,n}
|r_n^\pm-P(x_n^\pm)|.
\]

A collapse after actual-like dual is significant only if

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}.
\]

Interpretation:

\[
\boxed{
\text{actual-like main+dual collapse = Red warning candidate.}
}
\]

---

# 10. Stage F — \(E_2\) tolerance

Assume the canonical high-derivative error budget

\[
\max_{k\le2R+1}|\mathcal D^kE_2|\le \eta_{E_2}.
\]

Model this as row perturbations

\[
M\mapsto M+\delta M,
\qquad
\|\delta M_{j,\bullet}\|_2\le \eta_{E_2,j}.
\]

Define the determinant perturbation scale

\[
\eta_{E_2}^{\det}
=
\sup_{\delta M}
\frac{|\det(M+\delta M)-\det M|}
{\prod_j\|M_{j,\bullet}\|_2}.
\]

A collapse is meaningful only if it remains small relative to the allowed perturbation model. More precisely:

- If

\[
\eta_W\lesssim \eta_{E_2}^{\det},
\]

then the collapse is within canonical error noise and is not decisive.

- If

\[
\eta_W\ll \eta_{E_2}^{\det}
\]

and remains after perturbation, then it is serious.

---

# 11. Adversarial expectation by stage

| Stage | Model | Expected collapse? | Interpretation |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| A | phase-relaxed | Yes | Negative control |
| B | one-parameter orbit | Plausible | Yellow if found |
| C | \(+B_2\) | Likely still yes if B only shifts target | Yellow |
| D | \(+\) formal dual | Unknown | Tests two-branch geometry |
| E | \(+\) actual-like dual | Unknown / decisive | Red candidate if persists |
| F | \(+E_2\) tolerance | Filters false positives | Red only if robust |

---

# 12. Stop-rule verdicts

## Collapse only in relaxed models

If collapse occurs in Stage A but not Stage B:

\[
\boxed{
\text{continue Wronskian route.}
}
\]

The one-parameter prime-log orbit is doing useful arithmetic work.

## Collapse on one-parameter orbit but killed by dual

If collapse occurs in main-only orbit but is killed by formal or actual-like dual:

\[
\boxed{
\text{continue Wronskian route; main/dual coupling is probably essential.}
}
\]

The next theorem should formalize dual obstruction.

## Collapse survives actual-like main+dual

If for increasing \(R\) one finds

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z},
\]

\[
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W},
\]

with

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}},
\]

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}\le Q^{C_\kappa},
\]

\[
\operatorname{diam}(\lambda\log n)\gtrsim R^{-C_x},
\]

and survival under

\[
+B_2,
\qquad
+\text{actual-like }D_2,
\qquad
+E_2,
\]

then:

\[
\boxed{
\text{pause the Wronskian proof route.}
}
\]

This would indicate the incidence theorem is false in a realistic canonical surrogate, or at least much deeper than currently formulated.

## No collapse found

If no collapse is found beyond phase-relaxed controls:

\[
\boxed{
\text{continue Wronskian route.}
}
\]

Then the actual packet-frequency one-parameter orbit is plausibly avoiding the bad incidence set.

---

# 13. Minimal report format

Each candidate should report:

\[
R,Q,\lambda,c,L,|\mathcal B|,\alpha,m,
\]

\[
\lambda u_0,\qquad
\lambda L,\qquad
\operatorname{diam}(\lambda\log n),
\]

\[
\eta_Z,\qquad
\eta_W,\qquad
\eta_{\rm sv},
\]

\[
\rho_{\rm rank},\qquad
\kappa_{\rm basis},\qquad
\eta_{E_2}^{\det},
\]

and the active stage:

\[
\text{relaxed},
\quad
\text{orbit},
\quad
+B_2,
\quad
+\text{formal dual},
\quad
+\text{actual-like dual},
\quad
+E_2.
\]

---

# 14. Final adversarial conclusion

Packet-frequency blocks repair the determinant-admissibility problem:

\[
\lambda\log n\asymp1
\]

and avoid the low-prime compression failure. But they do not remove the basic adversarial issue:

\[
\boxed{
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R
\text{ can still be small whenever the actual orbit approaches the determinant incidence set.}
}
\]

Thus the Wronskian route now has a clean empirical falsification test:

\[
Z_\alpha\approx0,
\qquad
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R\approx0,
\]

on packet-frequency blocks, with healthy rank, healthy conditioning, and survival under

\[
B_2+D_2+E_2.
\]

If that pattern persists with \(R\), the route should pause. If it does not, the incidence theorem becomes a credible next analytic target.

## Agent 5 / E — Packet-frequency determinant stress test: first executable design

Instruction applied: convert the packet-frequency determinant stress test into a first runnable numerical/surrogate protocol. The target is simultaneous smallness

\[
Z_\alpha(m)\approx0,
\qquad
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R(m,\alpha)\approx0
\]

on packet-frequency blocks, with healthy rank, conditioning, and node spread diagnostics. 

---

# 1. Packet-frequency block

Use ordinary scale:

\[
\lambda Q\asymp1,
\qquad
R\asymp \frac{\log Q}{\log\log Q}.
\]

Define active blocks:

\[
\mathcal B_{\rm pf}(c,Q,R)
=
\left\{
n:
cQ\le\log n\le cQ+\frac{Q}{R}
\right\},
\qquad 0<c<\frac12.
\]

For first executable version, restrict to primes:

\[
\mathcal B_{\rm pf}^{(p)}
=
\left\{
p:
cQ\le\log p\le cQ+\frac{Q}{R}
\right\}.
\]

Then

\[
x_p=\frac{\lambda\log p-\lambda u_0}{\lambda L/2}
=
\frac{2(\log p-u_0)}{L}
\in[-1,1],
\]

where

\[
u_0=cQ+\frac{Q}{2R},
\qquad
L=\frac QR.
\]

Diagnostics:

\[
\lambda u_0\asymp 1,
\qquad
\lambda L\asymp R^{-1},
\qquad
\operatorname{diam}(x)=2.
\]

Reject any block with

\[
|\mathcal B_{\rm pf}^{(p)}|<4(R+2).
\]

---

# 2. First-pass symbols

Use a main-only exact-like model first.

For \(p\in\mathcal B_{\rm pf}\), define

\[
c_p^+
=
\frac{\log p}{\sqrt p}
(\log p)^3
V_q^+(p/N_q),
\]

\[
z_p^+(\alpha)
=
\frac{\log p}{\sqrt p}
p^{-\alpha}
V_Z^+(p/N_Z).
\]

Then

\[
r_p^+(\alpha)
=
\frac{z_p^+(\alpha)}{c_p^+}
=
p^{-\alpha}(\log p)^{-3}
\frac{V_Z^+(p/N_Z)}{V_q^+(p/N_q)}.
\]

For the first runnable surrogate, take plateau weights:

\[
V_q^+=V_Z^+=1
\]

inside the block, giving

\[
r_p^+(\alpha)=p^{-\alpha}(\log p)^{-3}.
\]

Then add exact-like AFE weights in the next stage.

---

# 3. Stable basis

Build a discrete orthonormal basis

\[
\phi_0,\ldots,\phi_{R+1}
\]

on nodes \(x_p\), with weights

\[
w_p=|c_p^+|^2.
\]

Define the weighted design matrix

\[
\Phi_{pj}=\phi_j(x_p).
\]

Use weighted Gram-Schmidt or QR on the monomial/Chebyshev seed basis. Verify:

\[
\Phi^\ast W\Phi=I+O(\varepsilon_{\rm num}).
\]

Basis diagnostic:

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}
=
\kappa(\Phi^\ast W\Phi).
\]

Accept only if

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}\le Q^{C_\kappa}.
\]

For a QR-orthonormalized basis this should be near \(1\); if not, the block is numerically unstable.

---

# 4. Basis-normalized matrix \(M\)

## Main-only phase model

Let

\[
u_p(m)=e^{-im\log p}.
\]

Define

\[
M_{0\ell}(m,\alpha)
=
\sum_{p\in\mathcal B}
z_p^+(\alpha)\phi_\ell(x_p)u_p(m),
\]

and for \(0\le j\le R\),

\[
M_{j+1,\ell}(m)
=
\sum_{p\in\mathcal B}
c_p^+
\phi_j(x_p)\phi_\ell(x_p)u_p(m),
\]

with

\[
0\le \ell\le R+1.
\]

Then

\[
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R(m,\alpha)=\det M(m,\alpha).
\]

This is the first executable determinant.

---

# 5. Core diagnostics

## 5.1 Zero smallness

\[
\eta_Z(m,\alpha)
=
\frac{|M_{00}(m,\alpha)|}
{\left(\sum_{p\in\mathcal B}|z_p^+(\alpha)|^2\right)^{1/2}}.
\]

For two-branch models, replace numerator with the full \(Z_\alpha\)-row scalar and denominator with the combined branch norm.

## 5.2 Row-normalized determinant

\[
\eta_W(m,\alpha)
=
\frac{|\det M(m,\alpha)|}
{\prod_{j=0}^{R+1}\|M_{j,\bullet}(m,\alpha)\|_2}.
\]

Use log-determinants via SVD for numerical stability:

\[
\log \eta_W
=
\sum_{k=1}^{R+2}\log\sigma_k(M)
-
\sum_{j=0}^{R+1}\log \|M_{j,\bullet}\|_2.
\]

## 5.3 Singular-value collapse

\[
\eta_{\rm sv}
=
\frac{\sigma_{R+2}(M)}{\sigma_1(M)}.
\]

This identifies near-linear dependence.

## 5.4 Symbol-rank residual

Compute

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}
=
\inf_{P\in\mathcal P_{\le R}}
\max_{p\in\mathcal B}
|r_p^+(\alpha)-P(x_p)|.
\]

For the two-branch version:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}
=
\inf_{P\in\mathcal P_{\le R}}
\max_\pm\max_{n\in\mathcal B_\pm}
|r_n^\pm(\alpha,m)-P(x_n^\pm)|.
\]

Practical first pass: compute both \(L^\infty\) residual and weighted \(L^2\) residual:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank,2}
=
\min_{\deg P\le R}
\left(
\sum_p w_p|r_p^+(\alpha)-P(x_p)|^2
\right)^{1/2}.
\]

Accept healthy rank only if

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}
\]

or, numerically,

\[
\rho_{\rm rank,2}
\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}.
\]

## 5.5 Node spread

Record

\[
\operatorname{diam}(\lambda\log p)
=
\lambda L
\asymp R^{-1}.
\]

Reject if

\[
\lambda L<R^{-C_x}.
\]

## 5.6 \(E_2\)-determinant tolerance

Assume

\[
\max_{k\le 2R+1}|\mathcal D^kE_2|\le \eta_{E_2}.
\]

Estimate row perturbation sizes

\[
\|\delta M_{j,\bullet}\|_2\le \eta_{E_2,j}.
\]

A crude determinant perturbation bound is:

\[
\eta_{E_2}^{\det}
\lesssim
\sum_{j=0}^{R+1}
\frac{\eta_{E_2,j}}{\|M_{j,\bullet}\|_2}.
\]

A more robust Monte Carlo stress test: perturb each row by random complex vectors of norm \(\eta_{E_2,j}\), recompute \(\eta_W\), and record the upper envelope.

---

# 6. Staged executable models

## Stage A — Phase-relaxed negative control

Replace

\[
u_p(m)=e^{-im\log p}
\]

by independent phases

\[
u_p=e^{-i\theta_p}.
\]

Optimize over

\[
\theta_p,\alpha.
\]

Objective:

\[
\mathcal J_{\rm rel}
=
\eta_Z^2
+
\mu\eta_W^2
+
\nu\max(0,Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}-\rho_{\rm rank})^2
+
\omega\max(0,\kappa_{\rm basis}-Q^{C_\kappa})^2.
\]

Expected result:

\[
\eta_Z,\eta_W\text{ collapse.}
\]

Interpretation: harmless negative control.

---

## Stage B — One-parameter orbit

Impose

\[
u_p=e^{-im\log p}.
\]

Optimize over

\[
m,\alpha,c.
\]

Objective:

\[
\mathcal J_{\rm orb}
=
\eta_Z^2
+
\mu\eta_W^2
+
\nu\max(0,Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}-\rho_{\rm rank})^2
+
\omega\max(0,\kappa_{\rm basis}-Q^{C_\kappa})^2.
\]

Run:

\[
c\in\{0.05,0.1,0.2,0.3,0.4\},
\]

\[
R\in\{4,6,8,10,12,16,20,24\}.
\]

A Stage B collapse is Yellow:

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z},
\qquad
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W},
\]

with healthy rank and conditioning.

---

## Stage C — Add \(B_2\)

Use

\[
B_2(t)
=
-\frac18\Re\psi^{(2)}
\left(\frac14+\frac{it}{2}\right).
\]

Add row/column corrections:

\[
B_{j\ell}(m)
=
\phi_\ell(\mathcal D)\phi_j(\mathcal D)B_2(m)
\]

for the \(q''_{\can}\)-derived rows.

The first row \(G_0=Z_\alpha\) gets a background term only if the canonical \(Z_\alpha\) definition includes completed gamma contribution.

Expected: \(B_2\) shifts the target but does not kill collapse generically.

---

## Stage D — Formal dual

Add

\[
M_{0\ell}^{-}
=
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_-}
z_n^-(\alpha)\phi_\ell(x_n^-)e^{+im\log n},
\]

\[
M_{j+1,\ell}^{-}
=
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_-}
c_n^-\phi_j(x_n^-)\phi_\ell(x_n^-)e^{+im\log n}.
\]

Use a mirror first:

\[
c_n^-=\overline{c_n^+},
\qquad
z_n^-=\overline{z_n^+}.
\]

Test both node conventions:

\[
x_n^-=\frac{2(\log n-u_0^-)}{L^-}
\]

and folded coordinate

\[
x_n^-=
\frac{2\left(\log\frac{m}{2\pi n}-v_0^-\right)}{L^-}.
\]

If formal dual kills collapse, main/dual geometry may be genuinely relevant.

---

## Stage E — Actual-like dual

Replace formal dual symbols with gamma-weighted AFE folded symbols:

\[
c_n^-(m),
\qquad
z_n^-(\alpha,m),
\]

and exact-like weights

\[
V_q^-(n/N_q^-),
\qquad
V_Z^-(n/N_Z^-).
\]

This is the first serious model.

A persistent Stage E collapse is Red-candidate.

---

## Stage F — \(E_2\)-tolerance stress

Perturb \(M\) according to the derivative error budget.

If the collapse only exists within the determinant error envelope,

\[
\eta_W\lesssim \eta_{E_2}^{\det},
\]

classify as inconclusive.

If collapse remains well below threshold under allowed perturbations, classify as serious.

---

# 7. First runnable pseudocode

```text
for R in [4,6,8,10,12,16,20,24]:
    choose Q such that R ≈ log(Q)/loglog(Q)
    set lambda = 1/Q

    for c in [0.05,0.1,0.2,0.3,0.4]:
        u0 = c*Q + Q/(2*R)
        L  = Q/R
        B  = primes p with c*Q <= log(p) <= c*Q + Q/R

        if len(B) < 4*(R+2):
            continue

        x_p = 2*(log(p)-u0)/L

        build c_p_plus, z_p_plus(alpha)
        build weighted orthonormal basis phi_j on x_p
        compute kappa_basis

        if kappa_basis > Q^C_kappa:
            mark basis-degenerate
            continue

        for alpha in alpha_grid:
            compute r_p(alpha)
            compute rho_rank

            if rho_rank < Q^(-C_rank):
                mark rank-degenerate
                continue

            # Stage A: phase-relaxed
            optimize theta_p to minimize J_rel
            log best eta_Z, eta_W, eta_sv

            # Stage B: one-parameter
            scan m coarse
            refine local minima in (m, alpha)
            log best eta_Z, eta_W, eta_sv

            # Stage C: add B2
            add B2 corrections
            repeat Stage B

            # Stage D: formal dual
            add mirrored dual branch
            repeat Stage B

            # Stage E: actual-like dual
            add gamma-weighted dual branch
            repeat Stage B

            # Stage F: E2 tolerance
            perturb rows within eta_E2
            log robustness
```

---

# 8. Thresholds

For numerical experiments, use relative thresholds first:

\[
\eta_Z\le 10^{-8},
\qquad
\eta_W\le 10^{-8},
\]

then scale to \(Q\)-thresholds:

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z},
\qquad
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W}.
\]

Healthy diagnostics:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}},
\]

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}\le Q^{C_\kappa},
\]

\[
\lambda L\ge R^{-C_x}.
\]

A useful first set:

\[
C_Z=C_W=6,
\qquad
C_{\rm rank}=4,
\qquad
C_\kappa=4,
\qquad
C_x=2.
\]

These are diagnostic constants, not theorem constants.

---

# 9. Collapse classification

## Harmless relaxed collapse

Collapse only in Stage A.

Verdict:

\[
\boxed{\text{expected; continue Wronskian route.}}
\]

## Yellow one-parameter collapse

Collapse in Stage B or C with:

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z},
\qquad
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W},
\]

and healthy diagnostics.

Verdict:

\[
\boxed{\text{incidence theorem is nontrivial; inspect dual coupling.}}
\]

## Dual-killed collapse

Collapse in Stage B but not after Stage D/E.

Verdict:

\[
\boxed{\text{main/dual coupling may be the needed arithmetic obstruction.}}
\]

## Red actual-like collapse

Collapse survives:

\[
+B_2,
\qquad
+\text{actual-like dual},
\qquad
+E_2\text{ tolerance},
\]

with healthy diagnostics and persists as \(R\) increases.

Verdict:

\[
\boxed{\text{pause Wronskian route.}}
\]

---

# 10. Minimal report row

Each candidate should report:

\[
R,\ Q,\ \lambda,\ c,\ u_0,\ L,\ |\mathcal B|,\ \alpha,\ m,
\]

\[
\lambda u_0,\quad
\lambda L,\quad
\operatorname{diam}(x),
\]

\[
\eta_Z,\quad
\eta_W,\quad
\eta_{\rm sv},
\]

\[
\rho_{\rm rank},\quad
\kappa_{\rm basis},\quad
\eta_{E_2}^{\det},
\]

and model stage:

\[
\text{relaxed},
\quad
\text{orbit},
\quad
+B_2,
\quad
+\text{formal dual},
\quad
+\text{actual-like dual},
\quad
+E_2.
\]

---

# 11. Final stop-rule statement

Pause the Wronskian route only if there is a persistent sequence in \(R\) with:

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z},
\]

\[
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W},
\]

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}},
\]

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}\le Q^{C_\kappa},
\]

\[
\lambda L\gtrsim R^{-C_x},
\]

and survival under

\[
B_2+D_2+E_2.
\]

Otherwise, keep the ordinary packet-frequency Wronskian route alive.

## Agent 5 / E — First executable packet-frequency determinant stress test

Instruction applied: produce the first executable packet-frequency determinant stress test for simultaneous smallness

\[
Z_\alpha(m)\approx0,\qquad \widetilde{\mathcal W}_R(m,\alpha)\approx0
\]

with healthy rank, conditioning, and packet-frequency node spread. 

---

# 1. First implementation scope

Use the simplest executable model first:

\[
\mathcal B_{\rm pf}^{(p)}
=
\{p:cQ\le \log p\le cQ+Q/R\},
\qquad 0<c<1/2.
\]

Use ordinary scale:

\[
\lambda = Q^{-1}.
\]

For each block:

\[
u_p=\log p,
\qquad
u_0=cQ+\frac{Q}{2R},
\qquad
L=\frac QR,
\]

\[
x_p=\frac{2(u_p-u_0)}{L}\in[-1,1].
\]

Reject immediately if:

\[
|\mathcal B_{\rm pf}^{(p)}|<4(R+2).
\]

Track:

\[
\lambda u_0\asymp c,
\qquad
\lambda L=R^{-1},
\qquad
\operatorname{diam}(x)\approx2.
\]

This is now determinant-admissible; it avoids the low-prime compression failure.

---

# 2. Main-only surrogate symbols

First pass: plateau main branch.

\[
c_p^+
=
\frac{\log p}{\sqrt p}(\log p)^3
=
\frac{(\log p)^4}{\sqrt p},
\]

\[
z_p^+(\alpha)
=
\frac{\log p}{\sqrt p}p^{-\alpha}.
\]

Thus

\[
r_p^+(\alpha)=\frac{z_p^+(\alpha)}{c_p^+}
=
p^{-\alpha}(\log p)^{-3}.
\]

Later stages replace this with exact-like AFE weights:

\[
c_p^+
=
\frac{\log p}{\sqrt p}(\log p)^3 V_q^+(p/N_q),
\]

\[
z_p^+(\alpha)
=
\frac{\log p}{\sqrt p}p^{-\alpha}V_Z^+(p/N_Z),
\]

\[
r_p^+(\alpha)
=
p^{-\alpha}(\log p)^{-3}
\frac{V_Z^+(p/N_Z)}{V_q^+(p/N_q)}.
\]

---

# 3. Discrete orthonormal basis

Build a stable basis

\[
\phi_0,\ldots,\phi_{R+1}
\]

on the nodes \(x_p\), weighted by

\[
w_p=|c_p^+|^2.
\]

Executable method:

1. Build a Chebyshev seed matrix:
   \[
   A_{pj}=T_j(x_p),\qquad 0\le j\le R+1.
   \]

2. Weight it:
   \[
   \widetilde A_{pj}=\sqrt{w_p}A_{pj}.
   \]

3. QR factor:
   \[
   \widetilde A=QR.
   \]

4. Define:
   \[
   \phi_j(x_p)=\frac{Q_{pj}}{\sqrt{w_p}}.
   \]

Then numerically:

\[
\sum_p w_p\phi_j(x_p)\overline{\phi_k(x_p)}
=
\delta_{jk}.
\]

Conditioning diagnostic:

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}
=
\kappa(R)
\]

or directly

\[
\kappa(\Phi^\ast W\Phi).
\]

Healthy threshold:

\[
\kappa_{\rm basis}\le Q^{C_\kappa}.
\]

---

# 4. Basis-normalized matrix

For a phase vector \(u_p\), define:

\[
M_{0\ell}
=
\sum_{p\in\mathcal B}
z_p^+(\alpha)\phi_\ell(x_p)u_p,
\qquad
0\le \ell\le R+1,
\]

and for \(0\le j\le R\),

\[
M_{j+1,\ell}
=
\sum_{p\in\mathcal B}
c_p^+\phi_j(x_p)\phi_\ell(x_p)u_p.
\]

Then:

\[
\widetilde{\mathcal W}_R=\det M.
\]

Use two phase models:

### Phase-relaxed model

\[
u_p=e^{-i\theta_p},
\qquad \theta_p\text{ independent.}
\]

### One-parameter orbit

\[
u_p=e^{-im\log p}.
\]

---

# 5. Diagnostics

## 5.1 Zero smallness

For this matrix model:

\[
\eta_Z
=
\frac{|M_{00}|}
{\left(\sum_p |z_p^+(\alpha)|^2\right)^{1/2}}.
\]

If the first basis vector is not constant after orthonormalization, also compute the direct scalar:

\[
Z_{\alpha,\mathcal B}(m)
=
\sum_p z_p^+(\alpha)e^{-im\log p},
\]

\[
\eta_Z^{\rm direct}
=
\frac{|Z_{\alpha,\mathcal B}(m)|}
{\left(\sum_p |z_p^+(\alpha)|^2\right)^{1/2}}.
\]

Use the direct scalar for stop-rule classification.

## 5.2 Row-normalized determinant

\[
\eta_W
=
\frac{|\det M|}
{\prod_{j=0}^{R+1}\|M_{j,\bullet}\|_2}.
\]

Compute through SVD:

\[
\log\eta_W
=
\sum_{k=1}^{R+2}\log\sigma_k(M)
-
\sum_{j=0}^{R+1}\log\|M_{j,\bullet}\|_2.
\]

## 5.3 Singular-value collapse

\[
\eta_{\rm sv}
=
\frac{\sigma_{R+2}(M)}{\sigma_1(M)}.
\]

## 5.4 Symbol-rank residual

Compute:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank,2}
=
\min_{\deg P\le R}
\left(
\sum_p w_p|r_p^+(\alpha)-P(x_p)|^2
\right)^{1/2}.
\]

Also compute an \(L^\infty\)-style residual:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank,\infty}
=
\max_p |r_p^+(\alpha)-P_\ast(x_p)|,
\]

where \(P_\ast\) is the weighted least-squares minimizer.

Healthy rank threshold:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank,2}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}
\]

or, more strictly,

\[
\rho_{\rm rank,\infty}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}.
\]

## 5.5 \(E_2\)-determinant tolerance

Assume:

\[
\max_{k\le2R+1}|\mathcal D^kE_2|\le \eta_{E_2}.
\]

Approximate row perturbation scale:

\[
\eta_{E_2}^{\det}
\lesssim
\sum_{j=0}^{R+1}
\frac{\eta_{E_2,j}}{\|M_{j,\bullet}\|_2}.
\]

A candidate is not serious if:

\[
\eta_W\lesssim \eta_{E_2}^{\det}.
\]

A serious candidate must remain small after row perturbations within this tolerance.

---

# 6. Staged models

## Stage A — phase-relaxed negative control

Optimize over independent phases:

\[
\theta_p,\alpha.
\]

Objective:

\[
\mathcal J_{\rm rel}
=
\eta_Z^2+\mu\eta_W^2
+\nu\max(0,Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}-\rho_{\rm rank})^2
+\omega\max(0,\kappa_{\rm basis}-Q^{C_\kappa})^2.
\]

Expected result:

\[
\eta_Z,\eta_W\text{ collapse}.
\]

Classification:

\[
\boxed{\text{Harmless; confirms finite-dimensional adversary.}}
\]

---

## Stage B — one-parameter orbit

Optimize over:

\[
m,\alpha,c.
\]

Use:

\[
u_p=e^{-im\log p}.
\]

Objective:

\[
\mathcal J_{\rm orb}
=
\eta_Z^2+\mu\eta_W^2
+\nu\max(0,Q^{-C_{\rm rank}}-\rho_{\rm rank})^2
+\omega\max(0,\kappa_{\rm basis}-Q^{C_\kappa})^2.
\]

A Stage B collapse is Yellow:

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z},
\qquad
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W},
\]

with:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-C_{\rm rank}},
\qquad
\kappa_{\rm basis}\le Q^{C_\kappa}.
\]

---

## Stage C — add \(B_2\)

Use:

\[
B_2(t)
=
-\frac18\Re\psi^{(2)}
\left(\frac14+\frac{it}{2}\right).
\]

Add deterministic background terms to the \(q''_{\can}\)-derived rows. In the first executable surrogate, approximate:

\[
B_{j\ell}(m)
=
\phi_\ell(\mathcal D)\phi_j(\mathcal D)B_2(m).
\]

Because \(B_2\) is smooth and small at height, expected behavior is target-shift, not structural obstruction.

---

## Stage D — formal dual

Add a mirrored branch:

\[
M_{0\ell}^{-}
=
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_-}
z_n^-(\alpha)\phi_\ell(x_n^-)e^{+im\log n},
\]

\[
M_{j+1,\ell}^{-}
=
\sum_{n\in\mathcal B_-}
c_n^-\phi_j(x_n^-)\phi_\ell(x_n^-)e^{+im\log n}.
\]

First formal model:

\[
c_n^-=\overline{c_n^+},
\qquad
z_n^-=\overline{z_n^+}.
\]

Test two coordinate conventions:

\[
x_n^-=\frac{2(\log n-u_0^-)}{L^-},
\]

and folded saddle coordinate:

\[
x_n^-=
\frac{2\left(\log\frac{m}{2\pi n}-v_0^-\right)}{L^-}.
\]

If collapse is killed here, the next analytic target is a two-branch obstruction theorem.

---

## Stage E — actual-like dual

Replace formal dual with gamma/saddle weighted coefficients:

\[
c_n^-(m),
\qquad
z_n^-(\alpha,m),
\]

using exact-like AFE weights:

\[
V_q^-(n/N_q^-),
\qquad
V_Z^-(n/N_Z^-).
\]

This is the first Red-relevant stage.

---

## Stage F — \(E_2\) perturbation

For the best Stage E candidates, perturb rows:

\[
M\mapsto M+\delta M,
\qquad
\|\delta M_{j,\bullet}\|_2\le \eta_{E_2,j}.
\]

Recompute \(\eta_W\) under random and worst-direction perturbations.

Classify as serious only if determinant smallness survives.

---

# 7. First executable pseudocode

```python
for R in [4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 16, 20, 24]:
    Q = choose_Q_for_R(R)          # roughly R ~ log(Q)/loglog(Q)
    lam = 1.0 / Q

    for c in [0.05, 0.10, 0.20, 0.30, 0.40]:
        u_min = c * Q
        u_max = c * Q + Q / R
        u0 = 0.5 * (u_min + u_max)
        L = u_max - u_min

        B = primes_with_log_in(u_min, u_max)

        if len(B) < 4 * (R + 2):
            continue

        x = [2 * (log(p) - u0) / L for p in B]

        c_plus = [(log(p)**4) / sqrt(p) for p in B]

        # Build weighted discrete orthonormal basis
        Phi, kappa_basis = weighted_orthonormal_basis(
            nodes=x,
            weights=[abs(cp)**2 for cp in c_plus],
            degree=R+1
        )

        if kappa_basis > Q**C_kappa:
            continue

        for alpha in alpha_grid(R, L):
            z_plus = [(log(p)/sqrt(p)) * p**(-alpha) for p in B]
            r = [zp/cp for zp, cp in zip(z_plus, c_plus)]

            rho_rank = weighted_poly_residual(
                nodes=x,
                values=r,
                weights=[abs(cp)**2 for cp in c_plus],
                degree=R
            )

            if rho_rank < Q**(-C_rank):
                continue

            # Stage A: phase-relaxed
            best_relaxed = optimize_independent_phases(
                B, c_plus, z_plus, Phi, R
            )

            log_candidate("relaxed", best_relaxed)

            # Stage B: one-parameter orbit
            best_orbit = scan_and_refine_m_alpha(
                B, c_plus, z_plus, Phi, R, alpha
            )

            log_candidate("orbit", best_orbit)

            # Stage C: +B2
            best_B2 = add_B2_and_repeat(best_orbit)

            log_candidate("+B2", best_B2)

            # Stage D: formal dual
            best_formal_dual = add_formal_dual_and_repeat(best_B2)

            log_candidate("+formal_dual", best_formal_dual)

            # Stage E: actual-like dual
            best_actual_like_dual = add_actual_like_dual_and_repeat(best_B2)

            log_candidate("+actual_like_dual", best_actual_like_dual)

            # Stage F: E2 perturbation
            e2_result = perturb_rows_E2(best_actual_like_dual)

            log_candidate("+E2", e2_result)
```

---

# 8. Thresholds

Use diagnostic thresholds initially:

\[
C_Z=C_W=6,
\qquad
C_{\rm rank}=4,
\qquad
C_\kappa=4.
\]

Thus:

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-6},
\qquad
\eta_W\le Q^{-6},
\]

\[
\rho_{\rm rank}\ge Q^{-4},
\qquad
\kappa_{\rm basis}\le Q^4.
\]

For early numerical debugging, also report absolute thresholds:

\[
\eta_Z\le10^{-8},
\qquad
\eta_W\le10^{-8}.
\]

---

# 9. Candidate report row

Every logged candidate should include:

\[
R,\ Q,\ \lambda,\ c,\ u_0,\ L,\ |\mathcal B|,
\]

\[
\alpha,\ m,
\]

\[
\lambda u_0,\quad \lambda L,\quad \operatorname{diam}(x),
\]

\[
\eta_Z,\quad \eta_W,\quad \eta_{\rm sv},
\]

\[
\rho_{\rm rank},\quad \kappa_{\rm basis},\quad \eta_{E_2}^{\det},
\]

and stage:

\[
\text{relaxed},
\quad
\text{orbit},
\quad
+B_2,
\quad
+\text{formal dual},
\quad
+\text{actual-like dual},
\quad
+E_2.
\]

---

# 10. Stop rules

## Harmless

Collapse only in Stage A:

\[
\boxed{
\text{relaxed-only collapse: harmless.}
}
\]

Expected and useful as a negative control.

## Yellow

Collapse in Stage B or C:

\[
\eta_Z\le Q^{-C_Z},
\qquad
\eta_W\le Q^{-C_W},
\]

with healthy:

\[
\rho_{\rm rank},\quad
\kappa_{\rm basis},\quad
\operatorname{diam}(x).
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{one-parameter main-only collapse: Yellow.}
}
\]

This says the determinant incidence theorem is nontrivial.

## Useful positive signal

Collapse in Stage B/C but killed in Stage D/E:

\[
\boxed{
\text{dual-killed collapse: useful.}
}
\]

This suggests the exact main+dual structure may contain the missing arithmetic obstruction.

## Red

Collapse survives:

\[
+B_2,
\qquad
+\text{actual-like dual},
\qquad
+E_2
\]

and persists as \(R\) increases.

\[
\boxed{
\text{actual-like main+dual collapse: Red. Pause Wronskian route.}
}
\]

---

# 11. Final deliverable of the first run

The first run should answer:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Does packet-frequency determinant collapse occur beyond phase-relaxed controls?}
}
\]

Three useful outcomes:

1. **Only phase-relaxed collapse:** continue Wronskian route.
2. **One-parameter main-only collapse, killed by dual:** focus next proof sprint on exact main/dual obstruction.
3. **Actual-like dual collapse surviving \(E_2\):** pause Wronskian route and re-evaluate Gate 1 strategy.
